Employment: PF account statement (check EPFO portal: passbook.epfindia.gov.in)
Any written communications — emails, WhatsApp messages, letters
Any performance reviews or show-cause notices
Witness details (colleagues who can support your claim)
Cyber Crime: Screenshots of the fraudulent messages, emails, or social media posts (with timestamp visible)
Bank transaction receipts / UPI transaction IDs and screenshots
Fraudulent website URLs or app names
Contact numbers and email IDs used by fraudster
Original phishing email (forward as attachment to preserve headers)
Any OTPs or passwords that were compromised
Device details (phone/computer model) where the fraud occurred
Your bank passbook / statements showing debit
Social media account usernames of fake profiles
Property & Housing: Survey / land records from revenue department
Photographs of the property and any encroachment
Ration card / utility bills as proof of residence
Domestic Violence: Medical reports and hospital records for injuries
Threatening messages, emails, or voice recordings
Witness names and contact details (neighbours, family)
Any previous police complaints or court orders
Streedhan list (jewellery, gifts received at marriage)
Marriage certificate and children's birth certificates
Any written complaints to police station
Consumer Rights: Product packaging and batch number (if applicable)
Photographs or videos of defective product
Email / chat exchanges with company customer care
Delivery documents / order confirmation (for online purchases)
Bank / credit card statements showing payment
Any promises or offers made in advertisement (screenshot / printout)
Medical reports if health was affected by defective product
Criminal: FIR copy (you have the right to get a free copy)
Photographs of injuries, scene, or stolen items
Written communications related to the offence
Seizure memo from police of any items seized
Family Law: Income proof of both parties (salary slips, ITR)
Photographs and videos related to cruelty (if applicable)
Medical reports for domestic violence injuries
RTI: Copy of your RTI application (signed and dated)
Grounds for the information request (for appeal)
Documents showing you are affected by the matter
General: All relevant original documents and their photocopies
Written record of all incidents (dates, times, locations, witnesses)
All correspondence related to the matter (emails, letters, messages)
Names and contact details of witnesses
Bank statements or financial records if money is involved
